sls.tf
Home Documentation GitHub

TypeScript API Conversion Guide

Complete guide to convert a TypeScript Serverless Framework application to work with sls.tf

Overview

This guide demonstrates how to convert a production-ready TypeScript Serverless Framework application to use sls.tf for Terraform deployment. We'll use the excellent AWS TypeScript API template and LogRocket's tutorial as our reference implementation.

Source Project Analysis

Project Structure

The reference project has this structure:

Project Directory

aws-serverless-typescript-api/
├── src/
│   ├── functions/
│   │   └── todo/
│   │       ├── handler.ts
│   │       └── index.ts
│   ├── libs/
│   │   ├── api-gateway.ts
│   │   ├── handler-resolver.ts
│   │   └── lambda.ts
│   ├── model/
│   │   └── Todo.ts
│   └── services/
│       └── todosService.ts
├── package.json
├── serverless.ts
├── tsconfig.json
└── README.md

Key Features

  • TypeScript Support: Full TypeScript compilation with type safety
  • CRUD API: Complete Todo API with GET, POST, PUT, DELETE operations
  • DynamoDB Integration: Serverless-managed DynamoDB table
  • Middleware: Middy.js middleware for request processing
  • Local Development: serverless-offline and DynamoDB local for testing
  • ESBuild: Fast bundling and deployment

Conversion Plan

Phase 1: Project Setup

  1. Add sls.tf Module: Add sls.tf as a Git submodule to the project
  2. Initialize Terraform: Set up Terraform configuration files
  3. Update Dependencies: Ensure compatibility with sls.tf requirements
  4. Configure Path Resolution: Set up proper module paths

Phase 2: Configuration Conversion

  1. Update serverless.ts: Ensure sls.tf compatibility
  2. Add Environment Variables: Configure Terraform-specific variables
  3. Update IAM Roles: Let sls.tf manage IAM permissions
  4. Adjust Build Configuration: Optimize for Terraform deployment

Phase 3: Terraform Configuration

  1. Create main.tf: Basic Terraform configuration
  2. Configure Variables: Set up input variables for customization
  3. Configure Outputs: Export important resource outputs
  4. Set Up Environment: Configure staging/production environments

Phase 4: Deployment & Testing

  1. Local Testing: Verify functionality with sls.tf locally
  2. Infrastructure Planning: Review Terraform execution plan
  3. Deploy Infrastructure: Apply Terraform configuration
  4. API Testing: Verify all endpoints work correctly

Detailed Implementation Steps

Step 1: Install sls.tf as Hybrid Module

sls.tf works as both a Terraform module AND an NPM package. This hybrid approach ensures full TypeScript parsing capabilities while keeping everything in sync.

Install sls.tf locally for TypeScript parsing

# Navigate to your project root
cd /path/to/your-project

# Install sls.tf from Git repository for TypeScript parsing
npm install https://github.com/sls-tf/sls.tf.git

# Create main.tf for Terraform configuration
touch main.tf

# Create variables.tf for input variables
touch variables.tf

# Create outputs.tf for resource outputs
touch outputs.tf

Current Reality: Git-Based Installation

Two installations, one source - both from the same GitHub repository:

  • Terraform Module: source = "github.com/sls-tf/sls.tf" (downloaded during terraform init)
  • NPM Package: npm install https://github.com/sls-tf/sls.tf.git (for TypeScript parser)
  • Automatic Integration: Terraform automatically finds the locally installed parser
  • Version Consistency: Both use the same Git commit, so versions stay in sync

Dual Installation Example

# Terminal session showing both installations
cd /path/to/your-project

# 1. Install as NPM package (gets TypeScript parser with dependencies)
npm install https://github.com/sls-tf/sls.tf.git

# 2. Configure Terraform (downloads same repo as module)
cat > main.tf << 'EOF'
module "serverless_api" {
  source = "github.com/sls-tf/sls.tf"
  config_path = "${path.root}/serverless.ts"
  config_format = "typescript"
}
EOF

# 3. Terraform finds both installations automatically
terraform init
# ✅ Downloads Terraform module from GitHub
# ✅ Detects local NPM installation of TypeScript parser
# ✅ TypeScript parsing works!

Step 2: Configure serverless.ts for sls.tf

With the hybrid approach, you can keep your original serverless.ts file! Only minor adjustments are needed for sls.tf compatibility:

Updated serverless.ts (minimal changes)

import type { AWS } from '@serverless/typescript';
import { createTodo, getTodo, getAllTodos, updateTodo, deleteTodo } from '@functions/todo';

const serverlessConfiguration: AWS = {
  service: 'aws-serverless-typescript-api',
  frameworkVersion: '3',

  // Remove plugins that sls.tf handles differently
  // Keep only essential plugins
  plugins: ['serverless-esbuild'],

  provider: {
    name: 'aws',
    runtime: 'nodejs18.x',  // Updated runtime
    apiGateway: {
      minimumCompressionSize: 1024,
      shouldStartNameWithService: true,
    },
    environment: {
      AWS_NODEJS_CONNECTION_REUSE_ENABLED: '1',
      NODE_OPTIONS: '--enable-source-maps --stack-trace-limit=1000',
      // Add sls.tf specific variables
      TABLE_NAME: '${self:custom.tableName}',
      REGION: '${opt:region, self:provider.region}',
    },
    // Let sls.tf manage IAM roles automatically
    // iam: { ... } // Remove this section
  },

  // Functions remain exactly the same!
  functions: { getAllTodos, createTodo, getTodo, updateTodo, deleteTodo },

  package: {
    individually: true,
    exclude: ['node_modules/**']
  },

  custom: {
    esbuild: {
      bundle: true,
      minify: false,
      sourcemap: true,
      exclude: ['aws-sdk'],
      target: 'node18',
      define: { 'require.resolve': undefined },
      platform: 'node',
      concurrency: 10,
    },
    // Custom properties for sls.tf
    tableName: '${sls:customTableName}',
  },

  // Remove DynamoDB from CloudFormation - sls.tf will create it
  // resources: { ... } // Remove this section
};

export default serverlessConfiguration;

Key Changes for sls.tf Compatibility

  • Remove Development Plugins: serverless-offline, serverless-dynamodb-local
  • Remove IAM Configuration: Let sls.tf manage roles automatically
  • Remove CloudFormation Resources: sls.tf creates these as native Terraform
  • Keep Everything Else: Functions, handlers, and build configuration stay the same

Step 3: Create Terraform Configuration

main.tf

terraform {'{'}
  required_version = ">= 1.0"
  required_providers {'{'}
    aws = {'{'}
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    {'}'}
  {'}'}
{'}'}

provider "aws" {'{'}
  region = var.aws_region
{'}'}

# Reference the existing serverless configuration
module "serverless_api" {'{'}
  source = "github.com/sls-tf/sls.tf"

  # Required variables
  config_path    = "${path.root}/serverless.ts"
  config_format  = "typescript"  # ✅ Full TypeScript support!
  aws_region     = var.aws_region

  # Optional variables
  lambda_code_path = "."
{'}'}

# Outputs
output "api_gateway_invoke_url" {'{'}
  description = "API Gateway invoke URL"
  value       = module.serverless_api.api_gateway_invoke_url
{'}'}

output "function_names" {'{'}
  description = "Lambda function names"
  value       = module.serverless_api.function_names
{'}'}

output "function_arns" {'{'}
  description = "Lambda function ARNs"
  value       = module.serverless_api.function_arns
{'}'}

output "service_name" {'{'}
  description = "Serverless service name"
  value       = module.serverless_api.service_name
{'}'}

variables.tf

variable "aws_region" {'{'}
  description = "AWS region for deployment"
  type        = string
  default     = "us-east-1"
{'}'}

variable "environment" {'{'}
  description = "Environment name (dev, staging, production)"
  type        = string
  default     = "dev"
{'}'}

variable "resource_prefix" {'{'}
  description = "Prefix for resource names"
  type        = string
  default     = "serverless-api"
{'}'}

variable "tags" {'{'}
  description = "Tags to apply to all resources"
  type        = map(string)
  default = {'{'}
    Project     = "Serverless TypeScript API"
    Environment = "development"
    ManagedBy   = "sls.tf"
  {'}'}
{'}'}

Migration Considerations

Build Process Changes

  • ESBuild vs sls.tf: sls.tf handles the build process differently
  • Package Management: Adjust package exclusions for optimal deployment
  • File Structure: Ensure proper path resolution for imports

Configuration Adjustments

  • Plugin Removal: Remove serverless-offline and dynamodb-local plugins
  • IAM Management: Let sls.tf create and manage IAM roles
  • Resource Separation: Move CloudFormation resources to Terraform

Development Workflow

  • Local Development: Continue using serverless-offline for local testing
  • Infrastructure Testing: Use Terraform plan and validate
  • CI/CD Integration: Update deployment pipelines for Terraform

Testing Strategy

Local Testing

Continue using Serverless Framework locally

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start local development
npm run dev

# Test functions locally
npx sls invoke local -f getAllTodos

Step 4: Test Hybrid Installation

Test TypeScript parsing and Terraform configuration

# Navigate to project root
cd /path/to/your-project

# Verify sls.tf NPM installation
ls node_modules/sls-tf/
# Should show: scripts/, package.json, etc.

# Initialize Terraform (will auto-download module)
terraform init
# ✅ Terraform will find the locally installed TypeScript parser!

# Validate configuration (includes TypeScript parsing)
terraform validate

# Plan deployment (TypeScript config parsed automatically)
terraform plan

# Apply changes when ready
terraform apply

What Happens During terraform init

  • Module Download: Downloads sls.tf Terraform module from GitHub
  • Parser Detection: Detects local NPM installation of sls-tf
  • TypeScript Ready: TypeScript parser found with all dependencies
  • Configuration Parse: serverless.ts parsed automatically during plan

Integration Testing

Test deployed API

# Get API endpoint from Terraform output
API_URL=$(terraform output -raw api_gateway_url)

# Test GET all todos
curl -X GET "$API_URL/todo/"

# Test POST new todo
curl -X POST "$API_URL/todo" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title": "Test Todo from sls.tf", "completed": false}'

# Test GET specific todo
curl -X GET "$API_URL/todo/{id}"

Benefits of sls.tf Migration

🔒 Infrastructure as Code

All AWS resources managed through Terraform with proper state tracking

🚀 Production Ready

Built-in security, monitoring, and disaster recovery capabilities

🔄 Improved Workflow

Standardized deployment process with proper planning and validation

📊 Better Observability

Centralized resource management and monitoring

Troubleshooting

Common Issues

  • Import Resolution: Ensure TypeScript path resolution works correctly
  • Build Errors: Check ESBuild configuration compatibility
  • Resource Conflicts: Use unique resource names to avoid conflicts
  • Permission Issues: Verify IAM roles have necessary permissions

Migration Tips

  • Gradual Migration: Consider migrating one service at a time
  • Backup Configuration: Keep original serverless.yml as reference
  • Test Thoroughly: Test all functionality before production deployment
  • Monitor Costs: Track resource usage and costs after migration

Next Steps

After completing this migration:

  • Set up CI/CD pipelines for automated deployment
  • Configure monitoring and alerting
  • Implement proper logging and error handling
  • Add automated testing to the deployment pipeline
  • Document your sls.tf configuration for team members

References

  • Original TypeScript Template
  • LogRocket TypeScript Tutorial
  • TypeScript Support in sls.tf
  • Advanced Configuration Guide
